identity.name: Lexicon (E1 Edition)
identity.description: A vocabulary builder that feels like a premium editorial magazine, not a classroom tool. High contrast, typographic focus, and sophisticated motion.

shadows.button: shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)] dark:shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(255,255,255,1)] hover:translate-x-[2px] hover:translate-y-[2px] hover:shadow-[2px_2px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)] active:translate-x-[4px] active:translate-y-[4px] active:shadow-none transition-all

visual_enhancers.gradients: None. Use solid colors and texture.

Button.style: Sharp corners, heavy brutalist shadow or clean minimal ghost.

Input.style: Underlined or solid block. Avoid standard rounded inputs.
Input.default_class: rounded-none border-b-2 border-input bg-transparent focus:border-primary focus:ring-0 px-0

motion.hover_effects: scale-[1.01] or slight lift. Text color change.

abstract_geometric.desc: Black and white squares - use for abstract pattern or texture

empty_state.desc: Abstract geometric design - use for empty states or loading

history_bg.desc: Clock on building - subtle background for history/archive

universal_guidelines: Do not use gradients for text.
Always use the defined font stack. Playfair for Emotion, Inter for Information.
Use plenty of negative space. Double the padding you think you need.
Interactive elements must have clear hover states.
Ensure dark mode is high contrast (Obsidian/White) not muddy gray.
Use data-testid attributes for all interactive elements.
instructions_to_main_agent: Implement the design using the provided color palette and typography. Override default Tailwind config. Build components that respect the 'Swiss Editorial' aesthetic - sharp lines, bold serif headings, and asymmetric layouts. Use the 'visual_enhancers' for buttons (brutalist shadow) to give it a unique tactile feel.
